access.anon: May enter the demo only through the explicit /demo bootstrap URL while the production kill switch is enabled; there is no demo credential, database account, or demo button on the sign-in page.
access.user: Keeps the normal authenticated principal and live behavior; an existing signed-in session is never converted to demo mode.
access.admin: Keeps the normal admin principal and live behavior; admin capabilities are never simulated.
access.agent: Demo mode is unavailable to OAuth and MCP clients in the first release.

rules.principal-not-user: DemoPrincipal is a server-signed, short-lived, non-database principal with an opaque session identifier, issued-at time, expiry, and contract version. It never contains or impersonates a User ID.
rules.direct-bootstrap-not-login: GET /demo is an explicit bootstrap route protected by a production kill switch and rate limit. It is not linked from /signin and does not accept a username, email, password, OAuth callback, or account-selection parameter.
rules.public-reads-live: Public catalog and timetable reads may use the normal anonymous read models. Personalized dashboard, subscription, todo, and completion data comes from deterministic fixtures or a size-bounded signed demo overlay, never business rows.
rules.business-writes-prohibited: A demo request must be intercepted by an allowlisted command gate before Prisma, R2, Better Auth, OAuth provider code, webhook delivery, calendar-feed issuance, or any other external side effect.
rules.explicit-simulation: Every accepted demo mutation returns a machine-readable simulated flag and the UI maintains a persistent demo banner; simulated success must not be indistinguishable from a live write.
rules.initial-write-allowlist: The first vertical slice may simulate only section subscription add/remove. Todo, homework-completion, and preference simulation require separate contract and negative-side-effect coverage before joining the allowlist.
rules.forbidden-capabilities: Admin, OAuth client/consent/token, account linking/deletion, passkey, upload, public comment/description/homework, calendar feed, webhook, and secret-management operations fail closed and are never simulated.
rules.audit-only-durable-effect: The only permitted durable effect is a privacy-safe structured audit event containing an allowlisted operation name, route, request ID, timestamp, result, and opaque demo-session hash. Bodies, fixture values, tokens, cookies, IP addresses, user agents, and other PII are excluded.
rules.overlay-boundary: If continuity is required, v1 uses a signed cookie overlay with strict version, expiry, entry-count, and byte limits. Overflow fails visibly instead of spilling demo state into PostgreSQL, KV, Durable Objects, or local storage.
rules.ordinary-principals-unchanged: Anonymous, authenticated, admin, GraphQL, REST, OAuth, and MCP behavior outside the explicit demo principal remains unchanged and continues to use normal authorization and persistence.

fields: No demo credential or sign-in provider

rules.result: Add/remove returns simulated: true and updates only the bounded signed overlay.
rules.isolation: Negative tests prove zero calls to Prisma mutation methods, OAuth, storage, calendar issuance, and external effect adapters.
rules.audit: The command emits one privacy-safe structured demo audit event regardless of whether the simulated selection changed.

rules.clear: Exit clears the signed demo cookie and overlay, then returns to the anonymous home page.
rules.session: Exit never creates, deletes, or changes a Better Auth session.
